summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that advance review copies (galleys) of her book 'Weapons of Math Destruction' are now available, ahead of the September 6th publication date. She shares a photo of the proof copies she received and jokes about immediately celebrating with drinks. The post serves as both a book promotion and a lighthearted personal update, with links to pre-order from Amazon and Barnes & Noble.
key_points: Galleys are preliminary versions sent to reviewers and people who might provide blurbs or publicity
The actual book won't be available until September 6th
O'Neil has proof copies available to give away to visitors
The post captures a personal milestone moment in the book publication process
money_quotes: This pic is blurry because I immediately started drinking upon receiving the packages.
I know, I know, it's mean of me to show you this when the actual book isn't available until September 6th, but what can I say, I'm kind of mean.
Plus, if you come over I'll give you one, so I'm not that mean.
key_insight: A lighthearted announcement marking a personal milestone in the publication of what would become an influential critique of algorithmic bias.
